column4: Personal Scourge's own protection scope (excludes self-inflicted weapon damage); fails transfer — no recurring "self-inflicted damage" formula to generalize from; nearest 4.5, which owns prevention scope generally, not this card's specific exclusion

Portrait's own capacity-reveal/bled-this-turn condition fizzling the remainder of its effect; fails transfer — no recurring template for this effect chain; nearest 2.5, which does not cover conditional effect fizzling on this card

interaction specific to this named card pair ({Church of the Order of St. Blaise} + Powerbase: Barranquilla); fails transfer — no other pairing generalizes from it; nearest 1.12 (cards on cards / counters)
Powerbase: Berlin's own blood-source bookkeeping for its action; fails transfer — specific to this card's action; nearest 6.1, which does not own blood-source attribution
Powerbase: New York's own action-eligibility restriction (cannot steal from your own powerbase); fails transfer — specific to this card's special action; nearest 3.1
nothing owns effects that redefine "who counts as the acting vampire" in combat (eg. {Deep Song}); 4.1 (combat script) is nearest but does not claim actor-redefinition
!WORDING ruling text reads circular ("counts as a directed action burning an ally" restates itself); likely paraphrase artifact; labeled on the directed-action/ally-burn topic as written
Principia Discordia's own targeting clarification (zero-blood vampire remains a valid target); fails transfer — answers only whether this card can target such a vampire; nearest 3.1
same as .1, [ser] version of the same card's own targeting clarification; fails transfer; nearest 3.1
!TENSION:no-effect-plays dodge permitted despite having no effect against this weapon; reinforces the permission side (cf. Draba.3) against the prohibition-case rulings
Psyche!'s own retrieval eligibility via {Marthe Dizier}, reversing an earlier ruling; fails transfer — specific to this card interaction; nearest 1.11
Pulled Fangs' own counter-removal condition (two actions by any vampires); fails transfer — specific to this card's removal mechanic; nearest 3.5

cumulative/stacking effects gap (watch-list) — nothing owns "does not stack with similar effects for a superior discipline level" (cf. Betrayer.3, Camarilla Exemplary.1, same gap); 1.2 nearest per established convention, though this is negative-stacking

!WORDING sentence is awkwardly phrased ("the new controller chooses if the vampires gains blood"); read as: on control theft, the new controller decides whether The Rack's blood gain still triggers during their unlock phase
Reanimated Corpse's own pathos-counter mechanic on burn/return; fails transfer — specific to this card's counter type; nearest 1.12

sect-as-trait watch-list gap (cf. Galaric's Legacy.1) — precedence of a temporary sect override over a permanent sect change; 5.8 nearest (sect change effects on titles) but does not own override precedence itself
sect-as-trait watch-list gap — whether a sect-changing effect counts as "making this vampire anarch" for other cards' triggers; 5.8 nearest but does not own this classification question
Red Question's own burn-option restriction (blocked by a specific torpor/title vampire state); fails transfer — specific to this card's burn clause; nearest 5.8
Redistribution's own targeting restriction tied to Blood Brother + uncontrolled status; fails transfer — specific to this card/clan combination; nearest 6.7
Reformation's own no-valid-target restriction; fails non-obviousness — a card requiring a target trivially cannot be played absent one; nearest 3.1
Rego Motum's own restatement that prevention needs damage present to prevent; fails non-obviousness — prevention is inherently reactive to inflicted damage; nearest 4.5
